body.title: Gate habit updates on feedback source confidence to prevent low-quality signal corruption
body.result: closed — pe_chain coding mechanism decommissioned; ticket no longer relevant to reasoning-first direction
body.description:
Igor's habit refinement absorbs all observations without weighting source quality. A habit updated from a failed pe_chain run is not equivalent to one updated from a successful run with explicit Akien approval. Add source_quality field to habit update events: approved > successful_run > observed > inferred. Weight update magnitude by quality; prevent low-confidence observations from overwriting high-confidence habits. Mirrors SDAR paper's sigmoid gate pattern (strengthen positive-gap, attenuate imperfect-source).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/habit_update.py (or equivalent habit refinement code), wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (habit update calls)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/memory (source_quality as metadata tag, not new memory type), theigors/rules/coding
**Scope boundary:** In scope: source_quality tagging on habit update events; quality-weighted update magnitude; logging. Out of scope: retroactive reweighting of existing habits.
**Test plan:** Emit habit update from failed pe_chain run (source_quality=inferred); verify it does not overwrite an existing approved-quality habit. Emit approved-quality update; verify it wins.
